answer: Tamil verbs follow a pattern of ROOT + TENSE/MOOD MARKER + PERSON MARKER. The tense/mood markers are: Past: -tt- (sometimes -itt-), Future: -pp- (with consonant doubling), Negative: -y- (inserted before person marker). The person markers indicate the subject (I, you, he, she, we, they) and are suffixed at the end of the verb form.
